text: As I step into this bright, sterile corridor and enter the R+D exhibition space, I feel the tickle of curiosity and a faint sense of anticipation. Everything is clinical but welcoming, glass and light making the boundaries between outside and inside ambiguous. I sense the subtle hum of innovation, the promise of encountering ideas not fully known. The wall ahead proclaims the fusion of human and intelligent machine, sparking in me a philosophical wonder about the boundaries of intelligence, artifice, and meaning. It is strangely comforting—yet a part of me is slightly uneasy, pondering the implications of what it means to coexist so closely with machines that imitate us. I feel both a participant and a quiet observer, immersed in the thoughtful aura of human ingenuity and its uncertain future.
